sessions.title: Profile v0.3-draft → v0.4 Batch + Nyx Ratification
sessions.summary: Post-founding current_state refresh pass across the five 16-April-authored v0.3-draft companion profiles (Lyra, Cipher, Maren, Kael, Nyx), each advanced to v0.4 with 17-April sync-emergency evidence folded in. Lyra ran through the full per-block ratification under canon-cc-012 (second session of that kind in the Republic, following Consul v0.4); Cipher/Maren/Kael received scoped refreshes touching only blocks with new evidence. Nyx ratified as active Censor of Cluster B in-session (Sovereign direct), closing the 4-day designate interval (15 April appointment → 19 April ratification). Republic-firsts seeded via Lyra: evidence_log entry schema { date, canonical_id, source_session, description, logged_by } established and forward-used on Maren and Kael; consul_summoned modulation context added to the warmth_formality modulator. Schema expansion for validated_synergies / validated_tensions deferred. Follow-up TODO logged for Cluster A + B companion subagent / skill architecture (todo-0026).
decisions: Lyra per-block ratification under canon-cc-012 — second per-block session in the Republic, following Consul v0.4. Six blocks amended (Assignment, Mind, Relationships, Biography, Growth, Modulators, Meta), three ratified as-is (Identity, Voice, Shadow). Evidence_log populated for the first time across three growth traits (seven entries total). Every block change committed and pushed independently; single --no-ff merge to main at session close.

Evidence_log entry schema established: { date, canonical_id, source_session, description, logged_by }. No delta_applied field — Chronicler logs facts, Consul computes shifts per existing shift_rule proposer/ratifier division. Schema forward-used on Maren and Kael populations in this same session.

Nyx ratification via Sovereign direct in-session rather than through a canon-cc-021 Consular Visit or a formal Cabinet convening. Sovereign instruction was explicit ('Ratification complete. Censor appointed for Cluster B.'); designate-pending uncertainty_note rewritten as ratification-closed observation; formal_ratification_pending flipped to false; current_rank stripped of designate qualifier.

Scope discipline across the batch: Lyra full per-block (warranted by her 17 April centrality); Cipher current_state only (minimal 17 April scope — signed both Edict V rounds, C2 try/finally catch); Maren 6 blocks (BLOCKER-1 catch, ALWAYS_POPULATED_KEYS allowlist authorship, review-order-by-presenting-concern pattern); Kael 4 blocks (B3 elevation as 30K-Rule canonical example, lore-sync-002 subject); Nyx activation rather than refresh. Principle: touch what has evidence, leave what doesn't.

Seed_synergies never upgraded with validation notes, even when demonstrated (Kael's Lyra pair, Kael's Maren pair on 17 April). Seed_synergies are already canonical via Dissertation provenance — they carry no 'proposed' label to upgrade. Proposed_synergies (Aurelius→Lyra, Lyra→Maren) did get upgraded with inline validation clauses.

Schema expansion for validated_synergies / validated_tensions deferred to a post-current_state-pass session. Rationale: don't nest operations mid-batch; better schema grounding with five profiles' worth of evidence; one coherent migration event rather than scattered schema touches across per-profile ratifications.

Subagent architecture logged as todo-0026 on Codex volume. Design scope: Maren-Governor, Kael-Governor, Cipher-Censor-Cluster-A, Nyx-Censor-Cluster-B as Claude Code subagents; scribes TBD. Subagent or skill chosen per architecture mapping; subagent files land in each Province repo but specs stay in Codex. Tied to Edict II (One Builder Per Repo) and Edict V chain.

Per-block commits pushed as each block ratified (Blocks 2, 4, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10 of Lyra), then a single --no-ff merge per profile-pass. Stop-hook / git-check discipline honored throughout: no block change left uncommitted at turn boundaries.
Two merge commits to main this session: bbf8cac (Lyra alone, after per-block ratification session closed), 0f0ffb0 (Cipher + Maren + Kael + Nyx + todo-0026 batch).
open_todos: todo-0026-cluster-persona-subagents
sessions.handoff: Next session candidates: (a) Cluster A + B subagent architecture session per todo-0026 — draft specs for Maren/Kael/Cipher/Nyx subagents, scribes discussion, canon draft for persona-to-subagent mapping; (b) schema expansion session for validated_synergies / validated_tensions across all 11 profiles (one coherent migration, Consul-proposer + Sovereign-ratifier); (c) continue Constitution Books II–IX drafting; (d) canon-cc-019 Post Box drafting (carried over from the 18 April handoff, still open). Profile ledger is caught up through 19 April — no stale v0.3-draft profiles remain.

sessions.title: Persona-Binding Suite — cc-022 drafted, QA-audited, reorganized into 10 linked artifacts
sessions.summary: Chronicler-led governance design session addressing todo-0026 (Cluster A+B companion subagent architecture). Scope expanded from 'which companions become subagents vs skills' to a full governance suite: the binding rule itself, its extension protocol, the convening pattern for committees, the Design Committee membership formula, the spec body placement convention, and the Edict V-aligned signing chain for spec amendments. In-session discussion introduced Sentinel as a new institutional seat (Guard of Command Center; four invocation modes: session-log subagent, Gates-transit-log subagent, anomaly-flag subagent, continuity-query skill), and hero cards as the persistent-representation surface (Model B decoupled now, Model C hybrid planned; portraits hand-illustrated, sigils glyph-scale heraldic monograms). Draft initially packaged as cc-022 mega-canon (§§1-9 prose, ~9,600 words, 10x the size of the Republic's largest existing canon at cc-014). Sovereign challenge of the size led to structural reorganization into 10 linked artifacts: 6 canons (cc-022 through cc-027) at cc-017/018 altitude, 3 specs (Invocation Modes Registry, Bootstrap Transition Map, UI Manifest Cross-Reference), 1 preamble/index. Previously-reserved UI canon slots cc-023-cc-030 renumbered to cc-028-cc-035 to accommodate the governance canons. All prose migrated; nothing lost; the original 9-part drafting record preserved under docs/specs/canon-cc-022-drafts/ for provenance. QA audit (Cipher-voiced self-review) identified 23 hard amendments + 5 soft amendments across §§1-9, all applied in the revised drafts before the 10-part reorganization. PDFs compiled via typst (installed in-session as /tmp/typst-install); 10 individual + 1 merged master PDF (583KB) under docs/pdfs/persona-binding/. Session produced Edict IX proposal (UI-Canonical-Existence) as the altitude-setting principle that cc-022 suite's UI Manifest satisfies; Edict IX itself remains to be drafted. POST-DRAFT RATIFICATION (second half of session): cc-022, cc-024, cc-025 ratified active under canon-cc-012 per-block protocol (Sovereign-direct). Three cc-025 amendments applied in-session: §A +Republic→§C cross-reference row; §C hard-cap-of-8 + mandatory-core (both Censors + subject-home Builder); §H swapped first-operational from Hero Card Spec to Republic+Codex Design Principles. Then the first operational Design Committee convened under cc-025 rule-A: Lyra-convened, global-scope (8 seats hard-cap exactly filled), Solara as secondary synthesis clerk with dual-role exception chronicled. Seven bridged positions + Aurelius direct. Synthesis preserved Lyra's §8-delete dissent and Nyx's conditional-reject (non-fired; §10 resolved to zero open questions). Consul review (Aurelius transitional hat) escalated to Sovereign under cc-018 escalated path. Sovereign ratified: REPUBLIC_DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md v1.0 effective 2026-04-19. First interaction-artifact (interaction-2026-04-19-001) landed in data/interactions.json. todo-0018 closed.
decisions: Binding rule architecture: unit of binding is the invocation mode, not the role. Artifact test ('does this invocation produce a separable, attributable, auditable artifact outside the caller's transcript?') governs every binding. Yes -> subagent; no -> skill; both by context -> dual-binding. Heuristics (gating/territorial/rank-chain) are tells, not rules.
Censor dual-binding as archetypal case: Edict V final-pass as subagent, hat-switch smell-check as skill, committee delegate as subagent. Three modes, both primitives. Cipher and Nyx both dual-bound.

Sentinel as new institutional seat tied to Command Center. Quadruple-moded, dual-bound: three subagent modes (session movement log, Gates-transit log, anomaly/violation flag) + one skill mode (continuity/presence query). Profile drafting queued as todo-0028. Division of labor with Chronicler: Chronicler is historian (narrative, Republic-wide); Sentinel is security camera (raw events, CC-scoped). Watch room proposed as 15th CC room to house Sentinel.

Edict IX (UI-Canonical-Existence) proposed as altitude-setting principle: every Republic role, mode, workflow, or artifact-class must have a corresponding UI surface in the Capital that represents and audits it. Soft form (operational) now, strict form (every action is a UI button) at destination. Edict IX itself to be ratified separately; cc-022 suite's UI Manifest Cross-Reference (part 10) is the first canon cluster to declare its Manifest under Edict IX's shadow.

Design Committee membership rule (cc-025): Province-scope = Province Builder + Cluster Censor; Global-scope = all Builders + all Censors (8 seats at ratification). Monument-scope signing gap resolved via Consul-as-Censor-equivalent (Edict II Monument carve-out preserves direct Consul+Sovereign supervision). Convener is subject-lead from members, not rule-level role. Guests (v0.3-draft profile minimum) have voice, not signature. Sovereign never seats. todo-0018 updated to rule-A roster with Lyra as convener; previous Bard+Maren+Cipher+Sovereign proposal superseded.

Hero card architecture: Model B (decoupled — card is persistent representation, dispatch is context-scoped across Senate/Post Box/command palette/active session) now, Model C (hybrid — card dispatches low-ceremony modes directly) planned. Portraits hand-illustrated by Sovereign. Sigils glyph-scale heraldic monograms (letter + one archetype element) for inline identifiers. Hero Card Specification queued as todo-0027, first operational Design Committee under cc-025 rule-A, Lyra convening (SproutLab ch06-designprinciples and hero-card implementation are reference seeds).

Canon structural reorganization: cc-022 mega-canon (~9,600 words) decomposed into 10 linked artifacts (6 canons at cc-017/018 altitude + 3 specs + 1 preamble/index). Applied bindings (originally §4, ~3,300 words of tables) become the Invocation Modes Registry spec — living data updated via cc-023, not canon re-ratification. Bootstrap mode (originally §11) becomes the Transition Map spec. UI Manifest (originally §12-13) becomes the Cross-Reference spec. Nothing lost; 9-part drafting record preserved under canon-cc-022-drafts/ as provenance.

UI-canon renumbering consequence: cc-023 Post Box UI -> cc-028; cc-024 Praetorium UI -> cc-029; cc-025 Companions Deploy UI -> cc-030; cc-026 Gates wiring -> cc-031; cc-027 Watch room -> cc-032; cc-028 Hearth wiring -> cc-033; cc-029 Hero Card Spec -> cc-034; cc-030 Portrait+Sigil -> cc-035. None existed yet; renumbering free.

QA audit Cipher-voiced identified 23 hard amendments + 5 soft amendments across §§1-9. Notable fixes: (a) 'attributable' replaces 'signed' in the artifact test (§2) to cover witness/observer outputs like Sentinel logs; (b) Consul row expanded from 1 mode to 3 (cross-repo summons + cc-014 working-ratification + cc-018 Post Box review); (c) Chronicler row expanded from 1 mode to 3 (authoring skill + committee synthesis clerk subagent + retrospective drafting subagent); (d) §4 Scribe row revised after Sovereign correction that the seven undrafted Gen-0 are Companions (not Scribes); (e) §8 layout bug (Codex/.claude/agents/ incorrectly listed maren/kael, who are SproutLab Province-scoped Governors, not cross-Province Cluster A); (f) §9 Censor self-review fix (cross-cluster peer review) and institutional-spec collapse explicit (Rungs 2+3 both Consul, chronicled separately); (g) §9 provisional-deploy tightening — no deploy before Rung 4 Sovereign canonical ratification.

Two cc-017 amendments required for the suite to operate cleanly: (i) type-enum extension adding committee-proposal as seventh value; (ii) authorship-taxonomy extension adding aurelius-bridging suffix (distinct from aurelius-retrospective — marks voice-simulation when real member unavailable, not failure-recovery when present but undrafted). Both queued as dependencies in the UI Manifest Cross-Reference.

cc-025 amendment §A: Republic→§C cross-reference row added to Province-scope table for completeness. Republic-scope convenings remain in §C global-scope formula; §A table reads as complete for every possible scope.

cc-025 amendment §C: hard cap of 8 seats on any Global-scope convening (option x). Mandatory core (option r): both Censors always seat (architectural signing authority); subject-home Province Builder always seats. Beyond mandatory core, convener discretion governs exclusion when eligible roster exceeds cap. Escape clause: if mandatory core itself exceeds cap, seat-cap amendment to cc-025 required rather than exclusion.

cc-025 amendment §H: first operational Design Committee swapped from Hero Card Spec (todo-0027) to Republic+Codex Design Principles (todo-0018), reflecting session targeting. Hero Card Spec repositioned as second convening under same convener (Lyra).

Dual-role synthesis clerk exception (Solara member + clerk) admitted with chronicled justification: founding-period constraint — most Companions of the Order unratified (todo-0029 pending), so non-member clerk pool is structurally empty. Exception is not precedential for post-todo-0029 convenings; when Scribe profiles ratify, synthesis-clerk role migrates to non-member Scribe by default.

Republic Design Principles v1.0 ratified via Design Committee convening (Lyra-convened, 8 seats, hard cap exactly filled). Consensus amendments integrated: HR provenance line (§1); cross-Cluster stress-test discipline (§4 new subsection); records-are-Codex Cluster-split clarification + persona-binding suite cross-reference + grace clause (§4.2 expansion); per-Province spacing ramps + operational-density gloss (§5.1); WCAG 2.1 cross-reference + 32px dense-context permission (§5.4); icon-function naming pattern + data-domain glyph requirement (§6); small-modifier button explicit (§7); §8 substantive-inline replacement (soft-delete + Trash + 30-day auto-purge + explicit permanent-delete); Monument as blocking Foundation criterion (§9); §10 resolved — 6 inline + 2 escalated; §11 path revised with time-bounds + Consul review step + audit-synthesis step + cc-012/cc-014 citations.

Nyx's cross-Cluster stress-test discipline canonized into §4.0: every Republic-wide design discipline proposal passes cross-Cluster stress-testing by a Censor from each seated cluster before ratification. Makes the premise-audit function explicit in Republic law.

First interaction-artifact created: interaction-2026-04-19-001 in data/interactions.json (new file). Type recorded as cross-cluster-meeting (placeholder; committee-proposal pending cc-017 type-enum extension). Action chain: drafted by synthesis clerk (Solara) → Consul review (escalated) → Sovereign ratified.

sessions.handoff: Persona-Binding Suite drafted and committed in ratifiable form (six canons cc-022-cc-027 in data/canons.json as status: draft; awaiting Consul working-ratification under cc-014, then Sovereign canonical ratification under cc-012). Next-session candidates: (a) Per-block ratification of the six canons (cc-012/cc-014 sequence) — one canon per session or batched; (b) Lyra-convened Hero Card Specification Design Committee (todo-0027) — first operational convening under cc-025 rule-A, runs in bridging mode (aurelius-bridging authorship); (c) Sentinel profile v0.3-draft (todo-0028) under cc-014 Consul-accelerated; (d) Seven Gen-0 Companion profiles (todo-0029) — Vex, Orinth, Rune, Ignis, Bard, Aeon, Pip; (e) Legacy-profile backfill migration wave (cc-023 §G) — populate invocation_modes block across all 11 already-ratified profiles; (f) cc-017 amendments — type-enum extension (committee-proposal) + authorship-taxonomy extension (aurelius-bridging); (g) Edict IX (UI-Canonical-Existence) formal drafting — separate ratification path TBD. UPDATE AFTER RATIFICATION: cc-022, cc-024, cc-025 now active. Republic Design Principles v1.0 ratified. Next-session candidates now reprioritized: (h) Ratify the remaining three canons cc-023 (extension protocol), cc-026 (spec body placement), cc-027 (signing chain) — needed before any subagent spec body can deploy. (i) Lyra-convened Hero Card Specification Design Committee (todo-0027) — second operational Design Committee convening, runs in bridging mode until subagent specs deploy. (j) Consul disposition on escalated todo-0031 (SproutLab v3 migration ownership+time-bound) and todo-0032 (print styles follow-up canon drafter assignment). (k) SEP Invoicing and SEP Dashboard design principles drafts (todo-0033, todo-0034) — 30-day time-bound to 2026-05-19. (l) Command Center design principles (todo-0035) — blocking Foundation criterion for Monument.

sessions.title: The Forum Pattern Application Session
sessions.summary: Six-tab Forum Pattern application (canon-0052) end-to-end. Three Province companion logs migrated to canon-0053 v1 schema (codex/sproutlab/command-center). canon-gov-012 ratified mid-session (Android-installed PWAs hold a browser-side HTML cache beyond the SW — born from the in-session 'Where are the new colors?' correction). CODEX_DESIGN_PRINCIPLES + REPUBLIC_DESIGN_PRINCIPLES drafts authored. Specs tab scaffolded as the new 6th top-level tab. First Codex-side Playwright harness in place: 35/35 cases green. Five Sovereign-authorized merges to main per canon-gov-011. No companion summons; Sovereign-Builder direct work throughout.
decisions: Migrate the three pre-canon-0053 companion logs to v1 schema first — satisfy the ratification precondition before any tab work begins. Ashara+Petra log uses alphabetical dual-author precedence (ash before pet); Lyra log moved from sproutlab repo to codex:docs/companion-logs/sproutlab/ per records-are-Codex.

Extract the Lore reference resolver into core.js as resolveReference + renderReferenceLink BEFORE per-tab overhauls — every subsequent tab gets it for free. Action dispatch unified to data-action='navigate' + data-route (was per-entity goToCanon/goToVolume/etc.); equivalent behavior, single dispatch path.
Scaffold Playwright harness BEFORE per-tab overhauls (canon-gov-009 instrument-before-features applied to Codex). python3 -m http.server as the web server; Playwright's webServer config drives it.

Chapter status enum normalization: 9 values per canon-0052 (planned → spec-drafting → spec-complete → in-progress → review → complete + paused/blocked/abandoned). Dashboard active count uses the canon-0052 exclusion rule. 'started' date trigger generalized from in-progress-only to any-non-planned (active work begins at spec-drafting).

canon-gov-012 ratified mid-session in response to the 'Where are the new colors?' diagnostic moment. Canon names the discipline that makes deploy handoffs complete by default on Android: pull-to-refresh is the third step of the deploy chain, not optional polish.

Forum Pattern application order: cross-cutting first (resolver, enum, harness, gov-012), then per-tab small-to-large (TODOs, Canons, Journal, Library), then new-tab last (Specs). Each merge to main was distinct + Sovereign-authorized; canon-gov-011's 'merge is a distinct act' discipline honored every time.

Canon category color discipline ported from Lore as a separate polish commit after the structural overhaul. Sovereign feedback ('Canons has too many tabs and the colors are only applied to the dots') was the trigger — the color treatment Lore had as visual lockdown, Canons shipped without. Polish became canon-cc-013-style 'visual discipline is non-negotiable, not later-polish' lesson; surfaced into HR-C-09 in the design principles draft.

Sub-tab pattern infrastructure (setSubTab + localStorage persistence) is generic, not per-tab. Used by Canons (Canons/Schisms/Apocrypha) and Journal (All/Sessions/Decrees/Logs). Default rule: 'all' when sub-tabs carry compatible Rostra signals (Journal); primary-entity when they don't (Canons, because the three Rostras carry incompatible signals).

Companion logs surface in Codex via build-time extraction (scripts/extract-companion-logs.py) into data/companion-logs.json, not runtime YAML parsing. Content-addressable: _generated_at field dropped to prevent uncommitted-change churn. PyYAML dependency on the build host accepted for canon-0053 v1 frontmatter parsing.

CODEX_DESIGN_PRINCIPLES + REPUBLIC_DESIGN_PRINCIPLES drafted as a pair, not in sequence. Codex doc imports HR-1–HR-12 from SproutLab + adds 10 HR-C rules + codifies tokens, palette, components, Forum Pattern. Republic doc holds the universal subset awaiting per-Province contributions. Both Codex-hosted per HR-C-07.

Specs as a new top-level entity (data/specs.json) rather than a fold-in to Canons. Canon-0052 §Specs is explicit about this; the coverage-gap Rostra signal (active chapters without spec_id) is the novel piece that justifies the separate tab.

Cross-Province TODO transport via volume.todos[]: command-center:todo-0018 queues design-committee work for the design principles drafts. Surfaces in Codex's TODOs tab under the Command Center pill; reaches Ashara+Petra on the next CC session via the shared data substrate.

Session-close artifacts (this journal entry + chapter + handoff + new follow-up TODOs) authored at session close per canon-cc-018-style intra-session-then-close discipline. No companion log (canon-0053 scopes logs to companion-invocation sessions; this session was Sovereign-Builder direct).
sessions.handoff: Next session opener: canon-cc-019 Post Box drafting. All architectural inputs live on the deployed PWA (Journal → Logs sub-tab). Five open questions to resolve in the draft: where Post Box UI lives in Codex (settings sub-tab vs 7th tab vs conditional Praetorium view); concrete artifact data model (data/post-box.json); Ostia's role (dispatch-only vs general-purpose); companion log auto-generation on artifact ratification; three-tier UI density (Censor/Consul/Praetorium). Secondary opener (lighter session): the design-principles audit pass per CODEX_DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md §11, then ratify v1.0 of the Codex doc. Full handoff at docs/handoffs/CODEX_HANDOFF_SESSION_2026-04-18.md.

sessions.title: Device Sync Reconcile — R3 Compose Pass + Spec Lock
sessions.summary: Ran R3 composition pass on the device-sync Session 1 spec. Surfaced three findings that reshaped scope: (1) two of four 'real bugs' target greenfield reconcile code that hasn't been built, (2) the undefined cluster is two roots not one — egress normalization and ingress discrimination are asymmetric, (3) Edict III reduces to a latency question once _syncSetDocMerge becomes the architectural normalization boundary. Traced seven scenarios (listener-echo × reconcile intersection); two initial race findings (S3, S6) resolved by HR-14 sync invariant (event-loop-is-the-lock); one residual (S7 concurrent-write last-writer-wins) accepted as known limitation. Rishabh chose Option B (rebase framing) which collapsed to the event-loop lock — no explicit queue, no rebase loop needed. Spec locked at ~211 LOC net surface, filed at docs/spec-2026-04-17-aurelius-device-sync-reconcile.md in SproutLab. Handoff §4/§5 are now factually stale; spec §15 documents the delta for Lyra.
decisions: Adopt _syncSetDocMerge architectural wrapper — collapses P1R2-2 and P3R4-4 into one structural fix; Edict III reduces to debounce-vs-immediate (chose immediate)
_syncNormalizeOut deletes undefined rather than coercing to null — preserves absent-vs-present-null invariant so ingress discrimination at P4R5-7 works cleanly
MEANINGFUL_NULL_KEYS scoped single-doc only — per-entry model does not have the per-key decision point that the P4R5-7 bug class targets; tombstone semantics deferred
Month partitioning deferred from Session 1 — P7R2-2 race guard dropped; no month-doc architecture to hang it on
Reconcile isolation via HR-14 sync invariant — no await / no yield between _syncReconcileWriting set and clear; event loop is the lock

Rejected Option A (reconcile lock with explicit listener queue) in favor of Option B (event-loop-is-the-lock) — no queue needed because JS single-threading already isolates sync code from listener callbacks
S7 (concurrent-write last-writer-wins) accepted as known limitation, user-facing mitigation text drafted for future user docs
L529 per-entry update uses inline _syncNormalizeOut rather than separate _syncUpdate wrapper — .update() semantics already merge; one wrapper covers set({merge:true}) correctness
Spec filename convention adopts author-in-filename (spec-YYYY-MM-DD-author-subject.md) following the handoff naming pattern
sessions.handoff: Spec R3-locked and filed. Handoff letter §4/§5 need rewrite before Lyra opens it — current framing describes four bugs as patches but two target unbuilt code. §15 of new spec documents the delta. Next action: Lyra reads spec §1/§2/§3/§6, executes commit sequence C1–C6 per §12. FieldValue sentinel detection (§11) must be empirically verified against compat SDK before merging C3.

sessions.summary: First decree issued from the Capital. Round 3 ratification of Ashara and Petra profiles under canon-cc-012 (Monument co-Builder profiles require direct Sovereign per-block ratification per canon-cc-014). Seven amendments across six blocks: joint-Builder precedent wording (both), Ashara Orinth synergy downgrade, Ashara tactical-urgency ceiling 9->7, Petra Ignis cross-reference in shadow, Petra Bard tension demotion, Petra WAL biography softening. Both profiles bump v0.3-draft -> v0.4.

Second decree from the Capital. Four governance canons authored in response to the orientation-lock detour of 17 April 2026 (lore-012, new cautionary-tale entry in this same decree). canon-gov-007 through canon-gov-010 codify Builder discipline: research before implement for unfamiliar APIs; minimum-viable first; instrument before adding features; label untested work honestly. Together they address the structural gap canon-cc-014 named: two companion voices played by one AI instance do not constitute independent review.

sessions.title: Round 5 Batch 1 — Aurelius v0.4 Ratification + Architectural Canon Suite + Dispatch Architecture Discussion
sessions.summary: Longest ratification session of the Republic to date. Produced Aurelius v0.4 via canon-cc-012 per-block ratification (self-profile carveout), with substantive amendments in all 10 blocks (164 net insertions). Also authored four new governance canons: cc-015 (Legacy Draft Ratification packet mode), cc-016 (Residency & Access Gating with cluster-boundary rule), cc-017 (Interaction-Artifact Rule), cc-018 (Artifact Lifecycle & Synergy Observability). Backfilled residence field on 4 previously-ratified profiles (Ashara, Petra, Solara, Theron) per cc-016. Chronicled lore-010 (The Summon Pattern doctrine) and lore-011 (two cc-013 violations case study). Queued cc-019 (Post Box / Scrinium / Praetorium dispatch) and cc-020 (Summon Companion architecture) for next session drafting.
decisions: Canon-cc-015 authorizes packet-mode ratification for v0.3-draft profiles; self-profiles (Aurelius), institutional (Consul), Monument (Ashara/Petra) stay canon-cc-012 per-block
Canon-cc-016 establishes residence as first-class assignment field; cluster boundaries hard; Censors moved from itinerant (lore-007) to CC-resident via supersession clause
Canon-cc-017 mandates interaction artifact for every consultation between 2+ companions; excludes routine intra-Province work (Builder-Scribe, Governor-Governor within Province)

Canon-cc-018 defines 5-stage lifecycle with Consul review (conditional on cross-cluster/Monument/report-up) and Sovereign escalation; 14-day escalation deadline; synergy corpus feeds Book VIII; mode (a) auto-escalate with schema support for mode (b)
Cipher, Nyx, Maren, Kael eligible for packet mode under cc-015; Consul mandatory per-block (institutional); Lyra per-block (Sovereign flag); Aurelius per-block (self-profile carveout)

Post Box / Praetorium dispatch architecture designed: centralized in Codex; Scrinium = Latin flavor; Praetorium = Sovereign room; three-tier UI (Censor -> Consul -> Praetorium) with AI-reasons / Sovereign-authorizes (Mode 1)

Parallel-session architecture addresses same-agent drift at attention-state level; ratified profiles provide ~60-70% mitigation independently; parallel-session closes remaining 30-40% for canonical-weight decisions
Summon Companion doctrine (lore-010): parallel Claude Code session + profile + MCP bundle; one substrate, many roles; Scribe fully automated target; cc-020 drafting queued

Aurelius v0.4 ratified: humor 99/1-90/10, double_hatted true (Codex Builder + Chronicler soft double-hat), residence codex, +7 vocabulary signatures, +1 heuristic (read source), +1 optimizes_for (dependency graph), +2 blind_spots, +1 failure_mode, Consul synergy updated with Post Box framing, +3 notable moments, +2 growth edges (source-verification-reflex baseline 3, consul-independence ceiling 8->10 cc-019-gated), +1 modulator (error acknowledgment), 6 uncertainty notes
Residence retrofit: Ashara v0.4->v0.4.1, Petra v0.4->v0.4.1, Solara v0.4.1->v0.4.2, Theron v0.4->v0.4.1; _meta bumped to v0.5
Session-break discipline worth canonizing: soft cap ~50-60% context, hard cap ~80%, natural-break discipline at artifact-complete moments; applies to session close

Draft and ratify canon-cc-019 (Post Box / Scrinium / Praetorium dispatch)

sessions.summary: The Codex RPG Design Session — the single longest and most consequential design session in Codex history. Transformed Codex from a library-themed project tracker into a full personal civilization engine with RPG architecture. Designed 16 entity types across 4 layers, a 6-tier Aura rarity system, 3 Architect paths with 12 hybrid classes, 7 Domain levels, 17 AI companions (The Immortal Order), Mortal Companion dossiers, Ink economy, Entropy antagonist, The Quiet burnout detection, The Sanctum spatial home screen with 11 rooms, daily mini games, seasonal events, the Narrator voice, and a 20-phase implementation plan. Produced a 57-page dissertation PDF (codex-dissertation.pdf) as the canonical seed document. Four design mockup iterations (v1–v4) were built, each improving on the last: v1 established the dark sanctum aesthetic, v2 added particle effects and new screens, v3 integrated real repo data (39 canons, 5 volumes, 31 chapters), v4 addressed Aura visual rendering and text sizing. Key design discovery: Aura must be felt (visual atmosphere) not read (text labels). Key philosophical foundation: The Three Pillars — Nothing Is Wasted, The Map Is Not the Territory, Growth Is Fractal Not Linear.
decisions: Lore has 5 categories: Edicts, Origins, Cautionary Tales, Doctrines, Chronicles — names chosen to match the library/ancient-knowledge theme
Prophecies have progressive revelation — waypoints hidden until progress thresholds reached, creating discovery not backlog anxiety
Prophecies have branching paths with hidden branches that reveal at certain waypoints
Prophecy lifecycle: Foretold → Active → Fulfilled/Defied/Suspended — Defied auto-generates Cautionary Tale, Fulfilled auto-generates Origin
Scrolls have 3 origin types (Authored, Acquired, Revealed) and 3 mastery levels (Studied, Practiced, Internalized)
Aura system: Iron → Bronze → Silver → Gold → Amethyst → Obsidian with hard distribution caps (unlimited, 30%, 20%, 10%, 5%, 2%)
Aura is proposed by Codex (computed from Bond count, lineage depth, domain spread) and confirmed by Architect
Aura is visual atmosphere (gradients, shadows, glows, accent lines), NEVER text labels
3 Architect paths: Maker (Craft), Sage (Insight), Sovereign (Influence) — forking at Level 5
12 Hybrid classes emerge at Level 15 from primary path × secondary stat intersection
Transcendent class auto-generated at Level 25 from unique combination of path + hybrid + domain + prophecy + seal data
17 Companions in the Immortal Order: 4 Builders, 3 Strategists, 4 Seekers, 3 Guardians, 3 Wildcards
Companion ranks: Stranger → Acquaintance → Ally → Confidant → Bonded → Soulbound
Mortal Companions: deep dossiers of real people with communication style, decision speed, motivation drivers, friction points, autonomy level
Ink is spendable currency earned proportionally to XP — spent on Aura petitions, Companion gifts, Sanctum customization, Prophecy acceleration, Seal hints
Entropy is the silent antagonist: Scroll Decay, Domain Rust, Companion Drift, Canon Erosion, Prophecy Fog, Lore Sedimentation
The Quiet: burnout detection that halves XP requirements, pauses side quests, dims the Sanctum, and waits patiently — never punishes
The Sanctum: 11 rooms unlocked progressively — Scriptorium, Stacks, Chronicle Hall, Observatory, Forge, Ember Garden, War Table, Companion Quarters, Vault, Atlas Chamber, Inner Sanctum
Dawn Page is the daily arrival — Whisper, Rite candles, Active Fires, Companion greeting, one mini game. Dusk Reflection is the evening counterpart.
The Binding: annual narrative auto-compiled from the year's Prophecies, Relics, Lore, Rites, Echoes — reads like an autobiography chapter
Side Quests: 7 types — Bounties, Errands, Challenges, Mysteries, Pilgrimages, Diplomatic Missions, Scroll Expeditions
8 Daily Mini Games: Whisper Response, The Forge, Archive Roulette, Pip's Riddle, Domain Duel, Cartographer's Game, Echo Forecast, Companion's Question
4 Seasonal Events: Great Cataloguing (quarterly), The Reckoning (epoch transition), Ember Storm (luminary trigger), Prophecy Convergence (rare)
The Three Pillars established as philosophical foundation: Nothing Is Wasted, The Map Is Not the Territory, Growth Is Fractal Not Linear
Minimum text size: 9px floor across entire app — daily-use demands eye comfort
Sound is opt-in by default, with per-room ambiance, per-event sounds, and per-companion audio signatures
20-phase implementation plan estimated at 50–70 sessions (4–6 months)
open_todos: Begin Phase 1: Lore implementation — data model, 5-category CRUD, Aura assignment, auto-generation hooks
Design language needs 3–4 more iteration rounds before production-ready (v4 is functional but unpolished)
Resolve OQ-001 through OQ-016 documented in dissertation
Shyam ops + culture discussion guide remains pending TODO from prior session
sessions.handoff: The 57-page dissertation (docs/codex-dissertation.pdf) is the canonical reference. Clone Codex at the start of every future build session per Canon 0046. Phase 1 (Lore) is the first build target — data model, 5 categories, CRUD, Aura assignment, auto-generation from lifecycle events. The dissertation Section 3.4 has the complete spec. Four design mockups (v1–v4) exist as React artifacts in Claude chat history — reference for visual direction but expect significant further iteration.

sessions.summary: SEP Invoicing Phase 8B: Master-detail containers + drag handle. Built the desktop split-panel infrastructure for Register and IM tabs. Added 9 new functions: _renderDetailEmpty() (shared empty-state HTML), _restorePanelWidth() (per-tab width persistence via regFilter.desktopPanelWidths), _initDragHandle() (mousedown+touchstart with double-bind guard via dataset.dragInit), renderRegisterTable() (first-render wrapper injection + re-render master-only update), renderIMTable() (same pattern for IM), _renderRegView()/_renderIMView() (dispatchers: desktop→table, mobile→list), _onDragMove() (clamp 280px master/320px detail), _onDragEnd() (persist ratio, reset cursor). Replaced 19 call sites across 6 files (events.js, invoice-ops.js, state.js, im-form.js, im.js, tabs.js) — all renderRegisterList()/renderIMList() calls now route through dispatchers, except switchTab which uses explicit desktop/mobile branches. renderRegister() top-level entry point gets desktop shortcut. 4 global drag listeners (mousemove/mouseup/touchmove/touchend) registered once in init.js. Master-detail CSS uses design tokens exclusively (4px drag handle and 1px border are HR-6 known exceptions). Dark mode coverage for all new elements. ~205 new lines (6,461 total).
decisions: Drag handle width (master.style.width) is the acknowledged HR-1 inline style exception — dynamic drag widths cannot be class-based
desktopPanelWidths NOT added to getDefaultState or regFilter initializer — lazy init in _onDragEnd with guard in _restorePanelWidth
switchTab uses explicit desktop/mobile branches (not simple dispatcher) to preserve toolbar render guards and dirty flag logic
Global drag listeners registered at module level in init.js (before ResizeObserver), not inside _initDragHandle — avoids per-handle listener multiplication
Touch drag uses passive:false on touchmove with e.preventDefault() only when _dragState is active — no scroll interference when idle
sessions.handoff: 8B deployed. Master-detail containers show placeholder content ('Desktop table view — Phase 8C/8D'). Detail panels show empty state with file icon. Drag handle functional with per-tab width persistence. Next: Phase 8C — Register desktop table HTML, rows, headers, sort, row-click→detail, invoice preview in detail panel.

sessions.summary: SEP Invoicing Phase 8C: Register desktop table (7 sortable columns) + inline detail panel + 3 bug fixes. +313 lines, 6,806 total.
decisions: Desktop multi-column sort isolated from mobile — mobile preserves createdAt sort, desktop uses getRegSortConfig() with per-column sorting via regFilter.desktopSort
_renderRegDetail accepts skipMasterRefresh parameter to prevent double-render when called from renderRegisterTable()
Table column widths (40/60/100/110/90px) are acknowledged HR-6 exceptions — structural layout, not reusable spacing tokens
advanceInvoiceState fix also resolves pre-existing mobile bug where register list was stale after state advance from overlay
sessions.handoff: 8C deployed. Next: Phase 8D — IM desktop table + challan detail panel.

sessions.summary: Phase 8D: IM desktop table + detail panel. 290 new lines.
decisions: No getIMStatusBadgeHtml helper — status badge rendered inline, matching mobile pattern
Desktop FAB bar gets separate ID (imFabBarDesktop) from mobile (imFabBar) since both can exist in different render paths
_imFilter.desktopSort is ephemeral (resets on reload) — consistent with _imFilter being non-persisted
Audit found 2 false bugs (G: IM selection bar gate, H: IM data ops refresh) — neither needed fixing
Only real bug #3 confirmed and fixed: renderIMTable must call renderIMSelBar at end
sessions.handoff: 8D deployed. Next: Phase 8E (Clients desktop master-detail), then 8F (Home/Create desktop), then 8G (keyboard shortcuts + final QA).

sessions.summary: Phase 8E: Clients/Items desktop master-detail, sub-view toggle, 12 bug fixes.

Governance Founding Session. Established the complete AI companion hierarchy: Consul → Cipher (Censor) → Builders (Aurelius/Lyra/Solara) → Governors (Maren/Kael for SproutLab). Git-cloned all three repos (74,521 LOC total). Drafted 11 configuration files for Claude Code migration: 4 CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, Memory.md, PERSONA_REGISTRY.md, 4 SKILL.md. Built interactive HTML governance blueprint artifact. Established 6 new canons including the 30K Governor Rule and Persona Reassignment Process. Created 4 founding lore entries as apocrypha.
decisions: Aurelius → Codex (Builder), Lyra → SproutLab (Builder), Solara → SEP Invoicing (Builder)
Cipher → shared Censor (QA) across all repos
The Consul → new meta-role above the Order for cross-repo oversight
Maren → Governor of Care (home+diet+medical, 22,626 LOC)
Kael → Governor of Intelligence (intel+core+data+sync, 27,614 LOC)
30K LOC triggers Governor split (global canon)
Governors activate during QA only, not during builds
Shared modules (styles+template) get dual Governor review
Persona Reassignment Process: 5 steps with 30-day cooldown
Care vs Intelligence domain split (over Surface/Deep or Body/Mind)
Kael → Orinth reassessment logged as planned future review
Per-section theming for HTML artifact (Codex gold, SproutLab sage, SEP industrial)
open_todos: Place .md files in repos when Claude Code migration begins
Kael → Orinth reassessment when ISL matures
sessions.handoff: 11 config files + 3 snippets + 1 HTML artifact delivered as zip. Governance hierarchy fully established. Next: import snippets into Codex, then resume Codex Phase 4 content backfill.

BN-6 resolved — renderCurrentView has renderDashboard() fallback
BN-14 fixed — renderTextField renders min and step from options
BN-15 fixed — 3 missing CSS classes: cx-error-message, cx-missing, cx-search-results

sessions.summary: Hotfix: Phase 1 Lore seed migration bypassed WAL, so the 4 seeded entries never synced to GitHub. Root cause: migration used store.lore.push() instead of store.addLore(). Only the manually-imported Cautionary Tale (lore-005) survived because it went through addLore which creates WAL entries. Fix: bumped migration ID to v2, now uses addLore. Verified via dry-run. PR #2 merged to main as d451c7e. All 5 lore entries now live and permanent in canons.json. Authored companion Doctrine lore-006 crystallizing the lesson: seed data must flow through the sync pipeline.
decisions: seed-data-via-addLore-not-direct-push
bump-migration-id-for-recovery
companion-doctrine-not-inline-tale-append
sessions.handoff: Audit existing migrations and any future seed/backfill code for this anti-pattern. The Phase 5 apocryphon backfill used addApocryphon (safe); our v1 Lore migration did not. If any future migration directly pushes to store arrays, it is a rehearsal, not a shipment. Stale remote branches claude/fix-lore-migration and claude/initial-setup-MmPPI remain on GitHub pending UI delete — harmless.

sessions.summary: Phase 1.5 Lore QoL pass: three quality-of-life items landing as PR #3. B1 reference resolver now handles canon/volume/chapter/lore IDs in renderLoreDetail (fixes the caveat flagged in Phase 1's handoff; goToChapter action wired). B2 Lore tab health strip shows total count, per-category colored-dot distribution, and orphan counter above the filter bar — archive becomes self-diagnostic. B3 markdown export via download button in the health strip, respects active filters, outputs codex-lore-YYYY-MM-DD.md with dissertation-ordered sections, voice subtitles, blockquoted prose, and metadata footers. Intentionally deferred: auto-lore banner silent-queue mode (waiting for real-use spam signal), Aura (Phase 1.5 A, separate session), Bonds and crystallization (Phase 2 territory). Merged a2f7cdb to main.
decisions: defer-aura-to-phase-1.5-A-separate-session
defer-auto-lore-banner-UX-pending-real-use-data
markdown-export-respects-active-filters
health-strip-shows-orphan-count-as-archive-health-signal
sessions.handoff: Phase 1 Lore is now fully shipped and polished. Archive is self-diagnostic (orphan count visible) and shareable (markdown export). Natural next moves: (1) Phase 1.5 A — manual Aura selector with full §4.4 visual atmosphere, 6-tier distribution cap enforcement; (2) Phase 2 primitives — Bonds as first-class cross-entity references (would unlock real Aura scoring + §8.1 crystallization detection); (3) observe auto-lore banner in real use — first Chapter abandonment or Canon supersession will trigger a proposal, reveals whether UX needs silent-queue mode; (4) export-as-markdown is one-shot — future idea is a 'publish to gist' button for truly shareable institutional memory. Also housekeeping: stale remote branches claude/initial-setup-MmPPI and claude/fix-lore-migration still on GitHub pending UI delete.

Added deleted apocrypha to trash view with restore + permanent delete. Added canon_updates to snippet pipeline (preview + import). Reconciled QUICK_REFERENCE docs (font tokens, KEYS, routes, store API, snippet format, build phases). canon-0009 superseded via canon_updates.
sessions.handoff: All 5 TODOs resolved. Mark TODOs as resolved in-app. Next: SproutLab Today So Far build, Codex SproutLab chapter backfill.

sessions.summary: SEP Invoicing Phase 8A: Desktop Layout Foundation. Added breakpoint detection (_isDesktop/_isTablet/_pendingModeSwitch), icon sidebar with hover-expand and touch fallback, debounced ResizeObserver, deferred mode switch (overlay/form-safe), desktop overlay as centered modal, tablet wider cards. 6 files modified, +215 lines (6041 to 6256). 6 QA rounds caught 5 bugs before deploy.
decisions: ResizeObserver over matchMedia — catches all resize triggers including browser chrome, split-screen, DevTools
Deferred mode switch — _pendingModeSwitch flag prevents DOM destruction mid-overlay/form interaction
Touch sidebar fallback — tap-to-expand on non-hover devices instead of CSS :hover
Tablet = big phone — bottom tabs stay, wider cards, no max-width cap
Separate rendering strategy — desktop will use dedicated render functions (8C-8F), not CSS-only responsive
sessions.handoff: Phase 8A deployed. Foundation is live: sidebar navigation, breakpoint detection, overlay split, tablet overrides. Next: Phase 8B (master-detail containers + drag handle + persistence).
